ops.title: iOS focus deck: mirror untallied-step ticks + single tally rail
ops.description: The web SPA now renders untallied focus steps (note-only context runs, input-only steps — stepStatus null) as small muted ticks in the FocusProgress rail instead of fillable decision dots, and the SubmitBar tally strip renders only on the board view (new `showTally` prop, app passes `mode !== 'focus'`). See `web/src/components/FocusProgress.tsx`, `web/src/styles/focus.css` (.focus-dot.tick), `web/src/components/SubmitBar.tsx`. The iOS client mirrors the step walk in FocusSteps.swift and presumably has the same papercut pair (note-only steps counted as decision units; step total vs tally disagreement) — port the same design: ticks for untallied steps, one progress vocabulary per screen. Swift Testing for coverage, per ios/ conventions.

ops.text: iOS focus progress rail renders untallied steps (context/input-only) as visually distinct non-fillable ticks
Only one per-item tally rail is visible per screen in the iOS focus view
Swift Testing coverage for the tick classification mirrors web/src/components/FocusProgress.test.tsx cases
